frcrnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Current ratio
frcrnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: The current ratio, one measure of liquidity, indicates the extent to which the liquidation of current assets would be sufficient to cover current liabilities.  The value of this ratio will vary throughout the production cycle and by type of business.  Greater liquidity is indicated by higher ratio values.

frwcnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Working capital-to-expense ratio
frwcnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: Working capital is calculated by subtracting total current liabilities from total current farm assets. The ratio is calculated as working capital divided by total cash operating expenses.

frdanum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Debt/asset ratio
frdanum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: The debt/asset ratio is defined as total liabilities divided by total assets. This ratio indicates the risk associated with the operation's financial structure.

frroanum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Rate of return on assets
frroanum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: Rate of return on assets is defined as net farm income plus interest expenses minus estimated charges for operator labor and management, divided by total assets.  This ratio reveals the returns received by the farm operator for both debt and equity capital invested.

frroenum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Rate of return on equity
frroenum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: Rate of return on equity equals net farm income minus estimated charges for operator labor and management, divided by net worth.  This ratio indicates the relationship between net profits and equity of the farm business.  A negative return on equity is a relative measure of financial stress.  The absolute size of the ratio roughly measures the rate at which a farm business is adding to or consuming from its own capital stock.

fropmnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Operating profit margin
fropmnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: The operating profit margin is the ratio of operating profit to gross farm income, reflecting total funds available to finance the farm's ongoing operation. The numerator, operating profit, is equal to net farm income (gross farm income minus all cash and noncash expenses minus depreciation) plus any interest expenses paid minus estimated charges for all operator and household unpaid labor and management. The denominator of the measure, gross farm income, includes gross cash farm income, annual inventory change, and any nonmoney income.

frdcnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Term debt coverage ratio
frdcnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: This ratio is a variant of the term debt and capital lease coverage ratio recommended by the Farm Financial Standards Council. It measures income available for debt coverage relative to required debt service payments. It is defined as net farm income plus depreciation plus interest on term debt divided by annual scheduled principal and interest payments on term debt.

fratnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Asset turnover ratio
fratnum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: The asset turnover ratio, which is calculated as the farm production value divided by total farm assets, measures the income generated per dollar of assets used in production.

froenum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Operating expense ratio
froenum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: The operating expense ratio equals cash operating expenses divided by gross cash income of the farm business. This ratio measures the extent to which the cash income generated by the business is absorbed by the annual costs of production. The lower the ratio, the more effective the farm operation is in generating returns.

freconum__farm_business_financial_ratios.name: Economic cost-to-output ratio
freconum__farm_business_financial_ratios.description: Economic costs are calculated as total cash costs plus an allowance for depreciation, along with an imputed return to management and to unpaid labor of the operator and family labor.  The ratio is calculated as total economic costs divided by gross farm income.
